parameters.content:
##  🧠 Workflow Summary

**PageIndex Vectorless RAG Bot**


This workflow builds a vectorless RAG system using [PageIndex](https://pageindex.ai) — no embeddings, no vector database. PageIndex builds a hierarchical tree index (like a Table of Contents) from your PDF, and the LLM reasons over that tree to find precise answers.


---


**📄 Flow 1 — PDF Upload (Run Once)**
Send a PDF file to the Telegram bot. It downloads and indexes it on PageIndex cloud.

**💬 Flow 2 — Q&A (Runs Every Time)**
Send any question to the Telegram bot. It fetches all your indexed docs, passes them to PageIndex LLM reasoning, and returns a cited answer.


---

**🔑 Required Credentials**
- Telegram Bot Token (via @BotFather)
- PageIndex API Key (via dash.pageindex.ai)

**💡 How PageIndex Works**
Instead of vector similarity search, PageIndex builds a tree of section summaries. The LLM reads the tree, decides which sections are relevant, retrieves only those, and generates the answer — with page citations included.

## 📄 Flow 1 -> PDF Knowledge Upload
Run this flow by sending a PDF file to the Telegram bot. The PDF is downloaded and indexed on PageIndex cloud. Run once per document.

### 📎 Receive PDF Document
**Purpose:** Entry point for PDF upload flow. Listens for incoming Telegram messages that contain a file/document.

**Input:** Telegram message with a PDF attachment
**Output:** `message.document.file_id` used to download the file

**Note:** Only send PDF files here. Other file types will cause errors downstream.

### ⬇️ Download PDF File
**Purpose:** Downloads the PDF binary from Telegram's file storage using the `file_id`.

**Input:** `message.document.file_id` from Telegram Trigger
**Output:** Binary PDF file data

**Note:** Telegram stores files temporarily. This node fetches the actual file content before it expires.

### ☁️ Index PDF on PageIndex
**Purpose:** Uploads the PDF to PageIndex cloud. PageIndex builds a hierarchical tree index (TOC) using LLM - no vectors.

**Input:** Binary PDF data
**Output:** `doc_id` (e.g. `pi-abc123`) assigned to this document

**Note:** Processing takes 10–60s depending on PDF size. The doc is stored permanently.

## 💬 Flow 2 -> Q&A Chat
Run this flow by sending any text message to the Telegram bot. It fetches all indexed docs, sends the question to PageIndex LLM reasoning, and returns a cited answer to the same user.

### 💬 Receive User Question
**Purpose:** Entry point for Q&A flow. Listens for text messages sent to the Telegram bot.

**Input:** Telegram text message from user
**Output:** `message.text` (the question) and `message.from.id` (for reply routing)

**Note:** Any text message triggers this flow. The answer is always sent back to the same user.

### 📚 Fetch All Indexed Documents
**Purpose:** Retrieves the list of all documents already indexed in your PageIndex account.

**Input:** PageIndex API key (header)
**Output:** Array of document objects including `id`, `name`, `status`, `pageNum`

**Note:** Only documents with `status: completed` are ready for querying.

### 🔑 Extract Document IDs
**Purpose:** Maps the documents array into a clean array of `doc_id` strings for use in the chat API.

**Input:** `documents` array from PageIndex
**Output:** `doc_ids` array (e.g. `["pi-abc123", "pi-def456"]`)

**Note:** Passing multiple doc_ids allows PageIndex to reason across all your indexed documents at once.

### 🧠 LLM Reasoning over Document Tree
**Purpose:** Core RAG step. Sends the user question + all doc_ids to PageIndex. The LLM reasons over the hierarchical tree index to find and retrieve the most relevant sections.

**Input:** User question + `doc_ids` array
**Output:** LLM-generated answer with page citations

**Note:** No vector search involved. PageIndex uses tree traversal + LLM reasoning.

### 📤 Send Answer to User
**Purpose:** Delivers the LLM-generated answer back to the user on Telegram.

**Input:** `choices[0].message.content` from PageIndex response
**Output:** Text message sent to the user's Telegram chat

**Note:** Reply is routed using `message.from.id` so each user gets their own response.
